title: The Dead Letter Office
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A postal worker in the dead letter office discovers undelivered letters that describe events that haven't happened yet.
long_description:
Arthur Finch sorts undeliverable mail in the dead letter office of the Central Post Office — letters addressed to nowhere, to the departed, to people who never existed. It is quiet, lonely work. Then he opens an envelope postmarked next Thursday and finds a letter describing a lost cat that he later finds, exactly as written. More letters follow: love letters, warnings, confessions, all postmarked in the future, all true.

As Arthur pieces together the growing correspondence, he maps the letters and discovers they all point to the same location — St. Jude's Hospital. A disaster is coming. The letters might be the only warning. With the cryptic guidance of his supervisor, Postmaster Elias Graves, and the lingering voice of a woman named Clara Dean who worked the same room forty years ago, Arthur must decide whether to intervene in events that haven't happened yet — and face the truth that changing the future has consequences no letter can predict.

The Dead Letter Office is a mystery about time, connection, and the words that find us when we least expect them. It asks: if you could read a letter from the future, would you open it?
main_characters: Arthur Finch — A postal clerk in the dead letter office who discovers future-dated letters
The Letters — Undelivered messages from the future that describe events waiting to happen
Postmaster Elias Graves — Arthur's supervisor, who has kept the secret of the future letters for twenty-three years
Clara Dean — The postal clerk who worked the dead letter office before Arthur, now deceased but still writing
